dialogue.utterance: Unit 74, dispatch to a call at [CITY], Apt #H for unconscious/fainting/near-fainting. Priority one, hot transport intended.
Hi, I'm Alex with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
We found her on the couch unresponsive. She had something in her heart and brain placed recently. She was last okay 20 minutes ago.
Did anyone give her any medication or attempt CPR before we arrived?
No. She took her regular pills and then collapsed. I tried shaking her, but she didn't respond.
Let's check responsiveness. Can you tell me your name or the date?
No eye opening, verbal response, or motor movement. Patient is unresponsive to all stimuli.
No eye opening to loud voice or pain. Agonal breathing noted.
Pupils are 2 mm and equally reactive to light.
No vocalization or response to verbal prompts. Unresponsive to all stimuli.
She doesn't moan or speak. No verbal response at all.
Apply pain to shoulder. Any movement? None observed.
No motor movement. She remains completely unresponsive.
Agonal breathing present. No visible airway obstructions.
Airway cleared. NPA being placed for securing airway.
NPA is in place. High flow oxygen using BVM being administered now.
Breathing is agonal, approximately 5 breaths per minute.
Breathing effort minimal. Assisted ventilation strategies will be initiated.
No palpable central pulse. Blood pressure undetectable.
Skin is pink, warm, and dry. No cyanosis noted.
Confirming respiratory rate. Agonal breathing at 5 breaths per minute.
Still no palpable pulse. Prepare to begin compressions.
Blood pressure is undetectable. Patient remains pulseless.
Connecting 4-lead monitor. Rhythm documentation ongoing.
Current EKG displaying sinus bradycardia, early signs of PEA.
Pulse oximeter nonfunctional; oxygenation maintained with high flow BVM.
Blood glucose reading is 598. Recording in field notes and updating en route.
Continuous monitoring activated. Detects and reports any rhythm changes.
Continuous observation confirms PEA and no ROSC.
Initiating assisted ventilations with King airway. ETCO₂ monitoring now displaying.
Left External Jugular IV placed and now functioning.
IV flushed and patent. Ready for drug administration.
Administering 1 mg of epinephrine 1:10,000 via IV.
IV flows freely. Ensuring patency between counts.
Narcan available. Will give 2 mg if ROSC isn't observed.
Any medication or allergy history provided by family?
She had a device placed in heart and head recently. No known allergies or medications.
Any known drug use or changes before collapse?
None. We followed normal medication routine.
Continuous pulseless state. No improvement with current interventions.
Activating Protocol 2-7: General Cardiac Arrest.
Blood glucose remains 598. No impact on current interventions.
Monitor confirms ongoing PEA rhythm. No ROSC detected.
Respiration at 12 per minute. Improved with King airway in place.
Systemic blood pressure remains undetectable. Pulseless condition ongoing.
Still nonfunctional. Oxygen administered at full 15 LPM.
Narcan ready for use. No changes observed post-EPI.
No recovery. Narcan administered following ROSC check.
IV still patent and functioning. Medications delivered recently.
No further information available. Family provided updates only.
Transport destination is [COMPANY_NAME] EDTA, nearest ALS facility.
Patient has been placed on the Reeves cot and strapped with safety restraints.
Cot secured to ambulance stretcher with all standard safety measures.
Calling for IV bag and medication logs per provider transfer instructions.
Full care summation and history updating before log transfer to ED staff.
Hot transport initiated. Compressions and oxygen ongoing without pause.
Patient handed off to [COMPANY_NAME] staff. Protocols reviewed prior to hand off.
Mechanical ventilations at 4 seconds per breath. Still no spontaneous breathing.
Monitor showing continued PEA. No spontaneous rhythm returned.
All resuscitative efforts ongoing at [COMPANY_NAME]. Patient remains pulseless and apneic.
All transport equipment and medications reviewed and accounted for.
Continuous monitoring confirmed active through transport.
Full hand-off at [COMPANY_NAME]. No interruptions to vital signs or resuscitation efforts.
Patient remains in PEA throughout transport. No return to sinus.
ROSC not observed. Full handoff complete with documentation to ED staff.
All care and documentation aboard. Transport concluded safely.
No clinical changes during transport. Arriving at ER for further care.
All resuscitative measures ongoing. Patient in stable condition en route.
Respiratory rate still at 12. Normal for King airway security.
Skin condition remains pink, warm, and dry.
Autopulse ready for continued compressions. In line with protocol.
Rechecking glucose levels. Still reported at 598 mg/dL.
Monitoring continued and noted PEA rhythm throughout.
Initial respiratory rate was 5 per minute, agonal. Now at 12 per minute with assistance.
SpO₂ unreadable; oxygen delivery is full 15 LPM via BVM at this time.
Blood pressure remains undetectable. Still 0/0.
Skin has no change in color, no signs of pale pallor.
Pain assessment not applicable. Patient is unresponsive.
Rechecking blood glucose in the field. Still recording at 598 mg/dL.
EKG confirms no change to PEA. No ROSC recorded.
Narcan administered at 20:10. No improvement noted.
Cot and patient are secured. No movement concerns during transit.
Resuscitation ongoing with compressions and ventilations en route.
PEA rhythm continues on monitor. No sinus return.
Respiratory remains at 12 per minute with assisted King airway.
Blood glucose confirmed at 598. No change in trend.
High-flow oxygen at 15 LPM via BVM throughout transport.
IV maintained, clergy of line activity confirmed and patent.
Patient remains comatose. Unresponsive and apneic.
No signs of pallor or coldness in extremities or trunk.
Hot transport for ongoing ROSC efforts in progress.
Preparing full patient report with ePCR and drug logs at transfer.
Transfer to ED care is now complete with all resuscitation equipment.
No spontaneous breathing, no pulse noted throughout.
Continuously monitoring for rhythm or pulse change en route.
Patient remains stable with no major changes during transport.
All current interventions unchanged from initial field setup.
No improvement seen after Narcan and Epi during transit.
Patient in continued PEA. No return of spontaneous circulation.
Full report and ECG saved for ED medical team.
Final vitals collected at 20:05: SpO₂ 0, BP 0/0, R 12, Pulse 0, PEA rhythm.
ED staff assumed control at the cot. Handoff complete.
Transport concluded at receiving hospital as planned.
All care documented and transferred.
No delays or special incidents during transport.
Patient and equipment back aboard in transport mode.
Transport complete. All resuscitation efforts followed protocol.
All EMS services complete. No remaining calls.
Field notes compiled. Return to base ongoing.
All data submitted. Sign out to dispatch.
No injuries to crew during incident or recovery.
Patient EKG, vitals, and treatment all well-documented.
ePCR submitted and saved with full vitals and drug logs.
All field protocols performed without deviation.
No complications during resuscitation or transit.
Transport and interventions considered timely and appropriate.
All ePCR sent to dispatch for HQ review.
Unit 74 signed off from incident and returned to service.
